introSection: Leap the Dips was a wooden out & back roller coaster located at Cedar Point in Sandusky, Ohio, USA. It operated from 1912 until 1935.

sections.title: History
sections.content: The roller coaster was damaged during a storm in the winter of 1919. It was rebuilt in 1920 and the design was said to have "considerably changed".

fullText:
Leap the Dips was a wooden out & back roller coaster located at Cedar Point in Sandusky, Ohio, USA. It operated from 1912 until 1935.

The roller coaster was damaged during a storm in the winter of 1919. It was rebuilt in 1920 and the design was said to have "considerably changed".
